title: Notes on the Chinese — exclusivity observation
html:
TL;DR

One-line field note from early September 2024, deep in the china chapter and based out of shanghai: "they embrace exclusivity to a higher degree." Yahir is starting to characterize a national trait — Chinese social/commercial life prizes in-group access in a way that stands out vs. the U.S.

Why it matters in the graph

  • Earliest written instance of the exclusivity as chinese trait concept — flag for follow-up notes that might corroborate (membership clubs, gated lounges, guanxi business norms, brand status).
  • Pairs with the markers of development checklist drafted three weeks earlier (2024 08 31 determinants of advanced countries) — both are Yahir doing comparative-anthropology on the fly.
  • Companion to 2024 09 05 shanghai sidequests and 2024 10 22 coffee shops in shanghai, both of which show him probing where the cool-kid layer of china actually lives.

Open questions

  • Exclusivity of what? Clubs, brands, friend groups, business deals? The note doesn't say.
  • Does he keep coming back to this? Search later notes for "exclusive," "invite-only," "members only," "guanxi."

Wikilinks
